title: Microhomology-mediated end-joining Knock-In approaches to delete the allergenic domain of trout parvalbumin beta-1. Preliminary results in F0 animals and feedback" by Lebret et al. Open Research Europe: manuscript 18223.
author: Rescan, Pierre-yves
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Microhomology-mediated end-joining Knock-In approaches to delete the allergenic domain of trout parvalbumin beta-1. Preliminary results in F0 animals and feedback” by Lebret et al. Open Research Europe: manuscript 18223. 
 1- ARRIVE checklist 
 2- Supplemental data:. 
  Pages of the laboratory notebook containing PCR gel photographs. Replicon (1077 nt) corresponding to the wild type allele was found in all samples. Replicon (766 nt) demonstrating parvb1 exon 2 deletion was found in only one sample (page 178, lane 8 (sample 81)). Replicon (450 nt) demonstrating parvb1 exon2 swapping was never detected.   
  Pages 167, 169, 173, 174 (lanes 1-10), 178: (Gel photographs of PCR products obtained in experiments aiming to detect exon skipping)  
  Pages 168, 171, 172, and 174 (lanes 12-24): (Gel photographs of PCR products obtained in experiments aiming to detect exon swapping)
